reasoning: As of the October 2025 knowledge cutoff, no diplomatic agreement explicitly restricting solar geoengineering exists, and earlier UN efforts in 2024 (UNEA-6) failed to reach a consensus. The creation, drafting, and signing/ratification of a formal treaty ('commit as a matter of treaty obligation') by at least 10 countries typically takes years. With only two months remaining until the December 31, 2025 resolution deadline, it is virtually impossible for a newly proposed treaty to achieve 10 formal legal commitments in that timeframe.
batch_queries: Will at least ten countries have committed to any diplomatic agreement restricting solar geoengineering, before 2026?
